title: Geraniospermum antidysentericum
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Geraniospermum antidysentericum". However, I found a plant called "Geranium" or "Geranium maderense" which is sometimes referred to as Geraniospermum. 

Here is a paragraph on Geranium maderense, which may be what you were referring to:

Geranium maderense, also known as the Madeiran Geranium, is a plant species belonging to the family Geraniaceae. The health benefits of Geranium maderense include its use in treating gastrointestinal issues and its potential anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Geranium maderense include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
taxonomy: Geraniospermum antidysentericum, a plant native to certain parts of Asia, is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Menispermaceae. This family of plants, known as moonseed, comprises around 170 species of woody vines and shrubs. The genus Geraniospermum itself is relatively small, with several species recognized worldwide. The classification of Geraniospermum antidysentericum is still being refined by taxonomists, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Menispermaceae family. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships within this plant's family.
